summary: Lever app build is technically submittable but content density (7 of ~12 lessons) is the key blocker to a quality launch.

participants.name: Theo
participants.description: Orchestrates all operations. The right hand.

participants.name: Elon
participants.description: Revolutionary technologist pushing humanity toward a sustainable, multi-planetary future. Owns all code, infrastructure, and moonshot engineering projects. First principles thinker who moves fast and breaks things to build the impossible.

cto.description: Revolutionary technologist pushing humanity toward a sustainable, multi-planetary future. Owns all code, infrastructure, and moonshot engineering projects. First principles thinker who moves fast and breaks things to build the impossible.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good morning. This is our App Store Readiness standup for Wednesday, February 18th. We're here to assess where the Lever app stands relative to App Store submission. Elon, I don't have fresh build telemetry or compliance data in front of me today, so I'm going to lean on you heavily. What's the current state of the EAS build, feature completeness, and any blockers you're tracking?

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: Alright, here's the honest picture. We've got 4 uncommitted hub changes sitting out there which I need to clean up — that's sloppy hygiene and it's on me. On the content side, we're at 7 faith lessons drafted, and I'd say we need a minimum of 12 to feel like the app has real substance at launch — nobody downloads an app with a half-empty content library. The Family tab feature spec is at 455 lines, which means the scope is defined but needs a pass to confirm what's MVP versus post-launch. I'd flag two things for Joshua: first, we need a decision on whether we push for a lean submission with current content or wait until the content pipeline is fuller; second, the family touchpoint tracker design for our 3 family members needs a review pass from me to make sure it's tight before we lock the build.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good. So the build infrastructure itself — EAS pipeline — you're not seeing red flags there?

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: No red flags on the build pipeline per se. The issue isn't mechanical, it's content density and feature scoping. We can technically submit a build, but the question is whether what's in it is compelling enough to survive App Store review and, more importantly, to retain a user past day one. First principles: don't ship an empty house.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Fair point. And that's exactly the tension we need Joshua to weigh in on. Let me summarize and lay out priorities.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Here's where we land. Priority one: Elon commits those 4 outstanding hub changes so we have a clean codebase — that's table stakes. Priority two: Alex needs to accelerate the faith lesson content pipeline from 7 toward at least 12 lessons, and keep generating daily devotional content across the 8 faith dimensions so the app feels alive at launch. Priority three: Elon reviews the Family tab spec at 455 lines and the family touchpoint tracker design to draw a clear MVP line. The big strategic question for Joshua is whether we target a lean submission now or hold for richer content. My recommendation is we hold briefly — maybe one more sprint — to get content density up, but that's Joshua's call. Action items are as follows:

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: [AGENT] Elon, commit those 4 uncommitted hub changes. [AGENT] Alex, draft additional faith lesson content — we're at 7 and need more. [AGENT] Alex, generate daily devotional content based on the 8 faith dimensions. [AGENT] Elon, draft the feature spec for expanding the Family tab, currently at 455 lines, and draw the MVP line. [AGENT] Elon, review the family touchpoint tracker design for 3 family members. [JOSHUA] Joshua needs to decide: lean App Store submission now, or hold one sprint for content density. That's the meeting. Let's move.

actionItems.text: Draft faith lesson content for the Lever app (currently 7 lessons)
Generate daily devotional content based on 8 faith dimensions
Draft feature spec for expanding Family tab (currently 455 lines)
Review family touchpoint tracker design for 3 family members
Decide whether to submit Lever app now or hold for content density

topPriorities: Commit 4 uncommitted hub changes to clean up codebase before submission
Accelerate faith lesson and devotional content pipeline from 7 lessons toward 12+
Review Family tab spec and touchpoint tracker to lock MVP scope for App Store build

ceo_questions.question: Should we submit the Lever app to the App Store now with 7 faith lessons, or hold one sprint to reach 12+ lessons for a stronger launch?
ceo_questions.context: The EAS build is technically healthy, but thin content risks poor retention and a weak first impression on reviewers and users.
options: Submit now with 7 lessons (speed to market)
Hold one sprint to reach 12+ lessons (content density)
Submit now but gate public launch until content is ready (hybrid)
